title: Earthquake relief for families in Türkiye
story:
On 6 February 2023, a 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck the Kahramanmaras province of southern Turkey, levelling entire neighbourhoods across Hatay, Adiyaman, and Gaziantep. Our family home in the Islahiye district of Gaziantep collapsed within seconds. My wife, Fatma, and our three children survived by sheltering under a reinforced doorframe, but my mother, Halide Demir, sustained a fractured hip and was transferred to Gaziantep University Hospital for surgery on 9 February.

In the days that followed, we—along with roughly 2,400 displaced residents of Islahiye—were accommodated in temporary AFAD tents erected at the Islahiye District Sports Hall grounds. Temperatures dropped to minus-six Celsius during the nights of 10–14 February, and the supply of blankets and winter clothing ran critically short. Working alongside the Islahiye Dayanisma Dernegi (Islahiye Solidarity Association, registration no. 06-140-098), I coordinated the procurement and distribution of 180 thermal blankets, 320 kg of flour, 150 litres of cooking oil, 200 kg of dried lentils, and 90 hygiene kits between 12 February and 28 February 2023.

All purchases were made from Yilmaz Gida ve Temizlik Malzemeleri in Gaziantep city centre (invoice nos. YG-2023-0214 through YG-2023-0228). Distributions were witnessed by AFAD coordinator Ahmet Arslan (badge #TR-AFAD-0441) and logged in the association's distribution register.

The funds raised here will cover the outstanding invoice balance of 47,500 Turkish lira (approximately USD 2,500) owed to Yilmaz Gida, plus a projected second-phase purchase of 150 additional blankets and 300 kg of canned legumes for the families still in tents as of March 2023. Every expenditure will be receipted and shared with donors within 14 days of purchase. These families have lost everything; your donation ensures they are warm and fed while permanent housing solutions are arranged.
